_comment: Gauntlet safety wiring for Codex (FR-1.2, FR-7.3). The PreToolUse `command` is the same install-tolerant POSIX launcher around the gauntlet-judge-hook console script as Claude Code uses (see .claude/settings.json): it execs the real hook when installed, stays silent (exit 0) for a teammate who never set up Gauntlet, and fails closed (exit 2) only when the hook is missing during an active gauntlet run (GAUNTLET_RUN_ID set). NOTE (BOOTSTRAP-NOTES #10, ratified 2026-06-11): `codex exec` does NOT fire PreToolUse hooks on the pinned build (codex-cli 0.139.0) — the hook runtime is app-server/TUI-only. Codex's headless pre-execution control is therefore its SANDBOX (read-only for review steps, workspace-write for build steps), which `.gauntlet/config.yaml` sets per profile. This file is committable and forward-looking: it activates automatically if a future codex build fires exec hooks, at which point `gauntlet doctor` reports the firing change. The exact codex hooks schema is unverified on the pinned build (the hook never fires to exercise it); it mirrors the shared stdin-JSON / exit-code-2 / permissionDecision contract the hook client speaks.

hooks.command: command -v gauntlet-judge-hook >/dev/null 2>&1 && exec gauntlet-judge-hook || { if [ -n "${GAUNTLET_RUN_ID:-}" ]; then echo "gauntlet-judge-hook not on PATH during an active gauntlet run; failing closed" >&2; exit 2; fi; exit 0; }
